title: Commission opens formal State aid assessment of French support to new nuclear programme
content: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess whether public support that France plans to grant for the construction and operation of six new nuclear reactors is in line with EU State aid rules.

title: Wildfire preparedness in Europe: Turning innovation into operational capability
content: On 12 March, a CERIS workshop explored how innovative European technologies can strengthen wildfire prevention, preparedness and response – and how innovation procurement can bring them into operation.
snippet: On 12 March, a CERIS workshop explored how innovative European technologies can strengthen wildfire prevention, preparedness and response – and how innovation procurement can bring them into operation

title: Tax revenue rebounds in 2024 after decade-low in 2023
content: The Taxation Trends dataset provides a detailed overview of key taxation indicators, enabling analysis of tax systems from multiple perspectives—by tax type, economic function, government level, and implicit tax rates.

title: Commission issues guidance for pragmatic and proportionate rules on protecting wild birds
content: The Commission document will help Member States protect bird populations while reducing unnecessary administrative burden.
snippet: The Commission document will help Member States protect bird populations while reducing unnecessary administrative burden.

title: 2025 most destructive EU wildfire season on record
content: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.
snippet: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.

title: European Job Days: 15 years of connecting talent and opportunity across Europe
content: Looking to take your career beyond borders or considering hiring candidates from another European country? Then European Job Days are your ideal solution.
snippet: Looking to take your career beyond borders or considering hiring candidates from another European country? Then European Job Days are your ideal solution.

title: EU and Australia formally open negotiations on the association to Horizon Europe
content: Research and innovation news alert: Following the visit of President von der Leyen to Australia, the European Commission and Australia have today opened formal negotiations for the association to Horizon Europe, the EU’s flagship research and innovation programme.

title: Commission calls on EU countries to coordinate measures to ensure oil security of supply amid Middle East energy disruption
content: Considering the market volatility stemming from the conflict in the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the European Commission is calling on EU countries to make timely and coordinated preparations to secure the supply of oil and refined petroleum products in the EU.
snippet: Considering the market volatility stemming from the conflict in the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the European Commission is calling on EU countries to make timely and coordinat

title: €29 billion of EU funding mobilised to drive EU competitiveness: The Strategic Technologies for Europe Platform turns 2!
content: The Strategic Technologies for Europe Platform (STEP) celebrates its second anniversary with record achievements for the development and manufacturing of critical technologies in Europe.
snippet: The Strategic Technologies for Europe Platform (STEP) celebrates its second anniversary with record achievements for the development and manufacturing of critical technologies in Europe.

title: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €6 billion Italian scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen for the transport and industrial sectors.
snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €6 billion Italian scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen for the transport and industrial sectors.

title: Commission report shows progress in boosting Europe’s social economy
content: The Commission has published a report of its Action Plan for the Social Economy, reviewing its progress since 2021 and setting out further actions to strengthen the social economy across Europe.
snippet: The Commission has published a report of its Action Plan for the Social Economy, reviewing its progress since 2021 and setting out further actions to strengthen the social economy across Europe.

title: Webinar to boost EU-Eastern Partnership Research and Innovation Cooperation
content: The information and networking webinar to support cooperation with Eastern Partnership (EaP) Partner countries in Research and Innovation took place on 19 March 2026.
snippet: The information and networking webinar to support cooperation with Eastern Partnership (EaP) Partner countries in Research and Innovation took place on 19 March 2026.

title: Connecting classrooms with fusion research: Commission welcomes school teachers from across Europe to ITER
content: 60 secondary school teachers from across Europe visited the ITER site in Cadarache, France, to experience the forefront of fusion research.
snippet: 60 secondary school teachers from across Europe visited the ITER site in Cadarache, France, to experience the forefront of fusion research.

title: New framework to measure knowledge valorisation
content: The Commission published an expert study providing new insights into measuring knowledge valorisation activities across the European Research Area.
snippet: The Commission published an expert study providing new insights into measuring knowledge valorisation activities across the European Research Area.

title: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen
content: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen

title: New EU initiative bolsters investment in circular water systems
content: The newly established initiative will support public and private entities in developing and implementing circular economy investments in the water sector.
snippet: The newly established initiative will support public and private entities in developing and implementing circular economy investments in the water sector.

title: The Entry/Exit System will become fully operational on 10 April 2026
content: The new Entry/Exit System (EES) will replace passport stamping with digitally recorded entries, exits or refusals of entry of non-EU nationals coming for short stays. Travellers’ facial image, fingerprints and personal data from the travel document will be also recorded.
snippet: The new Entry/Exit System (EES) will replace passport stamping with digitally recorded entries, exits or refusals of entry of non-EU nationals coming for short stays. Travellers’ facial image, fingerp

title: EU funded project Digital Explorers connects young professionals with European tech companies
content: Digital Explorers is an initiative supported by the EU through the Migration Partnership Facility. Launched in 2019, this initiative helps to connect professionals from non-EU countries who have skills and talent in the digital sector with European tech companies.
snippet: Digital Explorers is an initiative supported by the EU through the Migration Partnership Facility. Launched in 2019, this initiative helps to connect professionals from non-EU countries who have skill

title: Commission allocates €21.5 million in emergency support to farmers in Bulgaria, Estonia and Hungary
content: Today, Member States endorsed the Commission\'s proposal to mobilise €21.5 million from the agricultural reserve to support farmers in Bulgaria (BG), Estonia (EE) and Hungary (HU) affected by adverse weather events during the 2025 growing season.

title: Outcome of the 14th WTO Ministerial Conference
content: On 29 March, the World Trade Organization’s 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) ended without Members reaching agreement on a final package of outcomes.
snippet: On 29 March, the World Trade Organization’s 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) ended without Members reaching agreement on a final package of outcomes.

title: Commission publishes guidance to support implementation of new packaging rules, for a more sustainable and competitive EU packaging sector
content: The aim is to help Member States effectively implement the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation.
snippet: The aim is to help Member States effectively implement the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation.

title: EUDIS Defence Hackathon Spring 2026: Meet the Winning Teams Driving Innovation in Airspace Defence

title: European Ocean Board convenes for first meeting to shape future of EU ocean policy
content: The European Commission has officially launched the European Ocean Board, a high-level expert group supporting the implementation of the European Ocean Pact, the EU’s strategy to better protect the ocean, promote a thriving blue economy and support coastal communities.

title: EDIP: Commission adopts €1.5 billion work programme to boost European and Ukrainian defence industry
content: The European Commission approved today a €1.5 billion work programme under the European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) to enhance and modernise Europe's defence industry, boost production capacity and secure technological advancement and resilience.
snippet: The European Commission approved today a €1.5 billion work programme under the European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) to enhance and modernise Europe's defence industry, boost production capacity

title: Green City Accord report highlights cities' progress towards 2030 sustainability goals
content: A new report reveals how 54 Green City Accord signatory cities are accelerating progress on their sustainability goals.
snippet: A new report reveals how 54 Green City Accord signatory cities are accelerating progress on their sustainability goals.

title: Circular systems can drive big reductions in cities’ freshwater use
content: A new study has shown that water utility companies could dramatically reduce raw water withdrawals without incurring large costs, through interventions that increase efficiency and circularity.
snippet: A new study has shown that water utility companies could dramatically reduce raw water withdrawals without incurring large costs, through interventions that increase efficiency and circularity.

title: AI data centre waste heat could be used for water purification and carbon capture
content: New research shows how data centres could become water-positive and carbon-negative, by using waste heat to power water purification and carbon capture – potentially reducing the environmental footprint of AI.
